Grant Cardone often says: If you negotiate like a chump, you get paid like a chump.
Here are some negotiation tips and tricks you need to know:
-- Do not be needy. Have walk away points and NEVER need to have this deal go
through. Want it to go through if it’s the right fit for both sides. Don’t force it.
-- One way to The key is not to put all your eggs in one basket, so to speak. You
ideally want to have multiple deals in negotiation at any time so that you can play them
off of one another.
-- Do NOT make the error of overreaching in your negotiations. Overreaching and
pressing for too much can blow your deal. If you want to negotiate like the wealthy do,
you need to strategize on how you get everything you deserve without overreaching.
-- Be sure you take into account saving face for the person opposite from you.
Negotiations are not just about money… they're about both parties feeling good about
the deal that’s going on.
-- Do NOT bully or posture to the person opposite from you. If you’re ever going to use
such an aggressive tactic, you should reserve it only for when you truly need it. If you
can avoid using this tactic, that’s typically best.
-- Avoid any competitive negotiations where you’re bidding against another person or
third party to acquire the same business.
-- You need to find out the non-money motivations of the seller.
-- Understand that emotions can run high… and the seller might get very emotional
during the negotiation process. After all, this business is their baby. Be understanding,
ask questions to understand what they're trying to accomplish and why, and try to find
a win-win after you fully understand them.
-- For your part, restrain your emotions and show no emotion while negotiating.
-- You can have a partner on your side of the table who can ask the tough questions or
make the hard requests. In that way, you can be the good guy in the negotiations.
You’ll need the answers to those hard questions they're asking on your behalf… but

the questions might cause friction… so after you get an answer, you can ease the
tension.
-- Do NOT try to get the best of the seller.
-- Think of your job as a buyer-negotiator as trying to help yourself by helping the
seller.
-- Be aware of gaps that just cannot be closed, and know your walk away point.
